# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

const ALLOWED_MODULE_HOSTS = [
  "deno.land",
  "esm.sh",
  "cdn.skypack.dev",
  "cdn.jsdelivr.net"
];

const ALLOWED_MODULE_PATHS = new Set(
  (Deno.env.get("ALLOWED_MODULES") || "").split(",").filter(Boolean)
);

const MODULE_CACHE = new Map<string, any>();
const MAX_CACHE_SIZE = 100;

interface ModuleConfig {
  path: string;
  method?: string;
}

function isValidUrl(urlString: string): boolean {
  try {
    const url = new URL(urlString);
    return url.protocol === "https:";
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function isAllowedModule(modulePath: string): boolean {
  if (ALLOWED_MODULE_PATHS.has(modulePath)) {
    return true;
  }

  if (!isValidUrl(modulePath)) {
    return false;
  }

  try {
    const url = new URL(modulePath);
    return ALLOWED_MODULE_HOSTS.some(host => url.hostname === host || url.hostname.endsWith(`.${host}`));
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function sanitizeModulePath(path: string): string {
  return path.trim().replace(/[<>'"]/g, "");
}

async function loadModule(modulePath: string): Promise<any> {
  const sanitized = sanitizeModulePath(modulePath);
  
  if (!isAllowedModule(sanitized)) {
    throw new Error("Module not in allowlist");
  }

  if (MODULE_CACHE.has(sanitized)) {
    return MODULE_CACHE.get(sanitized);
  }

  try {
    const module = await import(sanitized);
    
    if (MODULE_CACHE.size >= MAX_CACHE_SIZE) {
      const firstKey = MODULE_CACHE.keys().next().value;
      MODULE_CACHE.delete(firstKey);
    }
    
    MODULE_CACHE.set(sanitized, module);
    return module;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(`Failed to load module: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error"}`);
  }
}

function parseModuleConfig(configString: string): ModuleConfig[] {
  try {
    const configs = JSON.parse(configString);
    if (!Array.isArray(configs)) {
      return [];
    }
    
    return configs
      .filter(config => config && typeof config.path === "string")
      .map(config => ({
        path: config.path,
        method: typeof config.method === "string" ? config.method : "default"
      }))
      .slice(0, 10);
  } catch {
    return [];
  }
}

async function handleRequest(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  
  if (url.pathname === "/health") {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ status: "healthy" }), {
      headers: { "content-type": "application/json" }
    });
  }

  if (url.pathname !== "/execute") {
    return new Response("Not Found", { status: 404 });
  }

  const moduleParam = url.searchParams.get("module");
  const configParam = url.searchParams.get("config");
  const envModules = Deno.env.get("DEFAULT_MODULES");

  let modulesToLoad: ModuleConfig[] = [];

  if (configParam) {
    modulesToLoad = parseModuleConfig(configParam);
  } else if (moduleParam) {
    modulesToLoad = [{ path: moduleParam, method: "default" }];
  } else if (envModules) {
    modulesToLoad = parseModuleConfig(envModules);
  }

  if (modulesToLoad.length === 0) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "No valid modules specified" }), {
      status: 400,
      headers: { "content-type": "application/json" }
    });
  }

  const results = [];
  const errors = [];

  for (const config of modulesToLoad) {
    try {
      const module = await loadModule(config.path);
      
      const methodName = config.method || "default";
      const fn = module[methodName];
      
      if (typeof fn !== "function") {
        errors.push({
          module: config.path,
          error: `Method '${methodName}' not found or not a function`
        });
        continue;
      }

      const context = {
        request: {
          method: req.method,
          url: req.url,
          headers: Object.fromEntries(req.headers.entries())
        },
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
      };

      const result = await fn(context);
      
      results.push({
        module: config.path,
        method: methodName,
        result: result
      });
    } catch (error) {
      errors.push({
        module: config.path,
        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error"
      });
    }
  }

  return new Response(JSON.stringify({
    success: results.length > 0,
    results,
    errors: errors.length > 0 ? errors : undefined,
    cached: MODULE_CACHE.size
  }), {
    headers: { 
      "content-type": "application/json",
      "x-content-type-options": "nosniff",
      "x-frame-options": "DENY",
      "strict-transport-security": "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains"
    }
  });
}

serve(async (req: Request) => {
  try {
    return await handleRequest(req);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Request handling error:", error);
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ 
      error: "Internal server error" 
    }), {
      status: 500,
      headers: { "content-type": "application/json" }
    });
  }
});
